client.city: Boston, MA; West Haven, CT; Fairfield, CT; Pomfret, CT; New Canaan, CT; Greens Farms, CT; New Haven, CT; Simsbury, CT; Norwalk, CT; Greenwich, CT; New York, NY; Buffalo, NY; Rochester, NY; Newark, NJ; Rumson, NJ; Lincroft, NJ; Philadelphia, PA; Lancaster, PA; Wilmington, DE; Centreville, MD; Chestertown, MD; Salisbury, MD; Washington, D.C.; Virginia Beach, VA; Norfolk, VA; San Diego, CA; San Francisco, CA; Salt Lake City, UT; Denver, CO; Albuquerque, NM; St. Louis, MO; Chicago, IL; Charleston, SC; Savannah, GA; Atlanta, GA; Fort Pierce, FL; Nashville, TN; Indianapolis, IN; Asheville, NC; Greenville, NC;
client.mission_statement: Horizons is a tuition-free, multi-year academic and enrichment program for students from under-resourced communities who lack access to expanded learning opportunities. In 2025, Horizons programs served over 7,500 students at 71 sites across 21 states.

opportunities.why_this_fits: THE MAXIMILIAN E AND MARION O HOFFMAN has a track record of supporting organizations like Horizons National Student Enrichment Program Inc. As a CT-based foundation with 95% in-state giving, they prioritize local organizations. Notably, QUINNIPIAC UNIVERSITY received $50,000 in 2023 for: HOFFMAN SCHOLARS FUND
opportunities.positioning_strategy: Lead with an unrestricted operating support request—75% of their grants are general support. Consider requesting $16,000 - $30,000 based on their $20,000 median grant. Emphasize your local CT presence and community impact.

contact.restrictions: ACTIVITIES SUPPORTED BY FOUNDATION GRANTS MUST BE CHARITABLE, EDUCATIONAL OR SCIENTIFIC UNDER THE PROVISIONS OF THE U.S. INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND THE TREASURY REGULATIONS.
contact.how_to_apply: PROPOSALS SHOULD SET FORTH A DESCRIPTION OF THE PROPOSED WORK; HOW THE WORK WILL BE CONDUCTED; THE NAMES AND CURRICULUM VITAE OF THOSE ENGAGED IN THE PROJECT; A DETAILED BUDGET, THE PROJECT'S PRESENT MEANS OF SUPPORT, THE PROJECT'S STATUS OF APPLICATIONS TO OTHER FUNDING SOURCES, AND THE PROJECT'S LEGAL AND TAX STATUS.
